_instructions: About section. Paragraphs are the narrative; statistics render as a monospace readout panel.
name: Ruchira Patil
paragraphs: I'm a software engineer at Amazon, working in the Appeals domain on the PXT Departures team. Most of my work lives where correctness and uptime matter — shipping features, migrating live case data between systems, and keeping services healthy through on-call rotations.

Before Amazon I interned at Oracle on developer tooling and product metrics, researched assistive technology at USC Viterbi on an NSF-funded project, and applied machine learning to crash-simulation data at Applus IDIADA. I finished my M.S. at USC in 2024 and my B.Tech at MIT-WPU in 2022.

I've published two peer-reviewed papers — a Springer chapter on customer-churn prediction and an IEEE survey on cloud-computing security — and I gravitate toward problems that sit between data and systems: pipelines, dashboards, and the instrumentation that tells you whether any of it is working.
